llm_recommendations.reason_for_recommendation: IPO subscription closing, tepid investor response with low subscription (0.03 times)
IPO subscription closing, tepid investor response with low subscription (0.41 times)
IPO subscription closing, good response from investors with overall subscription of 1.47 times, potential gains of upto 4% hinted by GMP trends
Significant negative historical returns: -6.03% (past day), -26.23% (past month), -42.61% (1 year), -24.11% (5 years)
Entered definitive agreement with a global bank in the USA to license software products and provide transition services, shares jumped 7%

Government cut excise duty on petrol and diesel, providing relief to OMCs amid rising crude prices; shares fell 1.6% due to broad sell-off despite initial gains. CLSA expects them to benefit less than upstream post-war.
Government slashed excise duty on petrol to Rs 3/litre and exempted diesel fully, to help oil marketing companies deal with rising global crude prices

Government cut excise duty on petrol and diesel, providing relief to OMCs amid rising crude prices; shares fell 0.4% due to broad sell-off despite initial gains. CLSA expects them to benefit less than upstream post-war.

Shares fell nearly 3% on the day, but remained 'almost flat' since the war began; CLSA expects it to benefit more than OMCs following any end-of-war news.
Jio-bp (fuel retailing joint venture of Reliance Industries and BP Plc) is incurring heavy losses but has not raised prices yet, indicating a potential strain.
CLSA expects ONGC to benefit more following any end-of-war news due to a tighter post-war oil and LNG supply-demand scenario.

Shares slipped 2.2% due to GST tax order (₹42.9 Cr) and West Asia conflict affecting financials; Motilal Oswal cut target price to ₹5,500 from ₹6,100 but reiterated 'Buy' due to long-term growth strategy.
